prompt: JSONPath query $.store.book[*].author fails when the book array contains null entries

turns.content:
<task>
JSONPath query $.store.book[*].author fails when the book array contains null entries
</task>
src/jsonpath/query.py:15: def query(data: Any, path: str) -> List[Any]:

1: from typing import Any, List
2:

5:     if not parts:

10:         results = []
11:         for item in data:
12:             results.extend(_resolve(item, rest))
13:         return results

15:         idx = int(part[1:-1])

17:     else:

19:

24:     return _resolve(data, parts)

arguments.reasoning:
Line 11: `for item in data` iterates over array elements. If an item is None (null in JSON), then line 12 calls `_resolve(None, rest)`. When rest has more parts (like 'author'), line 18 tries `data[part]` which is `None['author']` -> TypeError.

Fix: skip None/null items when iterating arrays. Also add a null check in _resolve for when data is None.

arguments.summary: Bug: _resolve doesn't handle None values. When array contains null, accessing `.author` on None raises TypeError. Fix: add null guard in _resolve.

next_steps.description: Add `if data is None: return []` at start of _resolve to safely handle null entries
Run tests to verify null entries are skipped gracefully

arguments.summary: Added `if data is None: return []` guard at start of _resolve. Null array entries are now safely skipped instead of raising TypeError. All 3 tests pass.
arguments.final_answer: Fixed JSONPath null handling in src/jsonpath/query.py. Added null guard `if data is None: return []` at the start of _resolve(). Null entries in arrays are now safely skipped during wildcard traversal. All 3 tests pass.
